summary: Validated import pipeline, import API route behavior, and project editor/preview compatibility via Vitest + UI smoke. Core import-related tests passed; runtime infra has startup misconfiguration for supervisor-managed services.

critical.issue: Supervisor is configured to start /app/backend and /app/frontend, but this codebase is a single Next.js app at /app. Both services stay FATAL unless started manually.

integration_issues.flow: Protected import workflow (upload DOCX from UI)
integration_issues.issue: Could not execute authenticated end-to-end import flow because test credentials file is missing and protected routes require Clerk auth.

action_items: Fix supervisor process definitions to run this Next.js app from /app instead of /app/frontend and /app/backend.
Add /app/memory/test_credentials.md with valid non-production credentials for protected import workflow testing.
(Code-review) Ensure all interactive elements across project workspace pages include data-testid, not just selected controls.
critical_code_review_comments: Infrastructure mismatch is currently the primary blocker for real E2E verification in this environment.
Not all interactive controls in workspace/editor metadata sections have data-testid, reducing deterministic Playwright coverage.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed vitest suites covering import parser, importer component, import route, preview canvas, workspace, and rich text editor: 43/43 tests passed. UI smoke at http://localhost:3000 passed only after manually running `yarn dev`; supervisor remains broken due wrong working directories. No DOCX fixture found in repository for direct file-based replay.
rca of the issue: Root cause of runtime blocker: supervisor configuration references legacy split-app paths (/app/frontend and /app/backend) that do not exist in this repo, so both services fail with ENOENT and stay FATAL. Reproduction: run `supervisorctl status` then inspect /var/log/supervisor/frontend.err.log and backend.err.log; both show 'couldn't chdir'. Mitigation: update supervisor commands/dirs to the current single Next.js app layout or provide an app-specific supervisor config for this project.
